title: Chari sambhu temple
tagline: Chari sambhu temple — spiritual town of Odisha

overview.short: Chari Sambhu Temple or Gandharadi Temple is located at the bank of river Mahanadi at Gandharadi village (Janhapank area) of Boudh district of Odisha, India.
overview.description: Chari Sambhu Temple or Gandharadi Temple is located at the bank of river Mahanadi at Gandharadi village (Janhapank area) of Boudh district of Odisha, India. It is famous for twin temples of Nilamadhava and Sidheswar. These temples display high quality architectural work. These temples were developed under the support of the Bhanja leaders of Khinjali mandala in ninth century AD. These temple attracts visitors throughout the year.

topPlaces.name: Harihara Deula
topPlaces.description: The Harihara Deula or Twin Temples are dedicated to Lord Shiva and Lord Vishnu. They are situated near the bank of Mahanadi river, at Gandharadi village in Boudh district of Odisha, India.

topPlaces.name: Subalaya
topPlaces.description: Subalaya is a town in the Birmaharajpur subdivision of Subarnapur district, Odisha, India. It is located on an island 822.738 acres (3.32950 km2) in size at the confluence of the Mahanadi river and Surubalijora.

topPlaces.name: Tebhapadar
topPlaces.description: Tebhapadar is a village of Birmaharajpur subdivision of Subarnapur district of Odisha, India. It is located on the bank of Surubalijora and Mahanadi River.

topPlaces.name: Gariamunda
topPlaces.description: Gariamunda is a village of Pitamahul gram panchayat of Birmaharajpur subdivision, Subarnapur district, Odisha, India. It is located on the side of the road connecting Birmaharajpur and Rairakhol town.

items.note: The Harihara Deula or Twin Temples are dedicated to Lord Shiva and Lord Vishnu. They are situated near the bank of Mahanadi river, at Gandharadi village in Boudh district of Odisha, India.

Subalaya is a town in the Birmaharajpur subdivision of Subarnapur district, Odisha, India. It is located on an island 822.738 acres (3.32950 km2) in size at the confluence of the Mahanadi river and Surubalijora.
Tebhapadar is a village of Birmaharajpur subdivision of Subarnapur district of Odisha, India. It is located on the bank of Surubalijora and Mahanadi River.

Gariamunda is a village of Pitamahul gram panchayat of Birmaharajpur subdivision, Subarnapur district, Odisha, India. It is located on the side of the road connecting Birmaharajpur and Rairakhol town.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chari sambhu temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chari sambhu temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chari sambhu temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (182 km) and the nearest railway station is Sambalpur Junction (70 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chari sambhu temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chari sambhu temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chari sambhu temple?
faq.a: Stays in Chari sambhu temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chari sambhu temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Harihara Deula, Subalaya, Tebhapadar, Gariamunda.
